meta.purpose: Landing page copy and structure research for sauna.guide buying guide funnel
meta.icp: Home sauna buyers ($3K-$15K+), active research phase, 47 tabs open, reading Reddit at 11pm

copy_frameworks.why: StoryBrand positions the buyer as hero and us as the trusted guide. PAS opens with the emotional pain of research hell. Together they match our brand voice perfectly: empathetic, warm, decisive.

frameworks_ranked.name: StoryBrand (SB7)
frameworks_ranked.how_we_use_it: Page skeleton. Customer is hero lost in sauna research. We are the guide who already did the work. Clear plan (quiz > results > email series). Call to action. Paint the success (warm sauna in your backyard) vs failure (another $8K mistake).
section_order: Value stack: 3 quick-hit benefits with icons
Problem: show we understand their pain
Guide: our credibility (tested X saunas, no brand deals)
Plan: simple 3-step path (Take quiz > Get your match > Buy with confidence)
Success vs Failure: what life looks like with/without our help

frameworks_ranked.name: PAS (Problem-Agitate-Solve)
frameworks_ranked.best_for: Hero section and email subject lines
frameworks_ranked.how_we_use_it: Opening hook only. Problem: You have 47 browser tabs open. Agitate: Every brand says they are the best. You are no closer to deciding than you were two weeks ago. Solve: We tested them. Take our 2-minute quiz.

sauna_guide_example.problem: You have been researching home saunas for weeks.
sauna_guide_example.agitate: Every brand says they are the best. Reddit threads contradict each other. You are further from a decision than when you started.
sauna_guide_example.solve: We spent 200+ hours so you do not have to. Take a 2-minute quiz. Get your personal shortlist.

frameworks_ranked.name: AIDA (Attention-Interest-Desire-Action)
frameworks_ranked.note: Less effective for long-form landing pages in 2025-2026. Works for paid social ads driving to the quiz.

quiz_funnel_structure.why_quiz: Quiz funnels convert at 30-50%, vs 5-10% for traditional lead magnets. Completion rates of 70-80%.
key_findings: Place email capture at question 3-4, not at the start. Increases completion by 40%.
Specific CTAs convert 52% better than generic ones. 'Discover your ideal sauna' beats 'Take our quiz'.
State the duration: '2 minutes to clarity' outperforms uncertain time by 31%.
Each answered question is a micro-commitment (Cialdini's consistency principle). By the email gate, giving their address feels natural, not forced.
Quiz-segmented email lists see 3x higher open rates.
Gating full results behind email is standard and accepted when the quiz delivers genuine personalized value.

landing_section.headline: Find your perfect home sauna in 2 minutes.
landing_section.subheadline: Answer 5 questions. Get a shortlist built around your space, your budget, and how you actually want to use it.

quiz_flow.example: Easy install, wood-fired, barrel style, etc.

quiz_flow.copy: Where should we send your results?
quiz_flow.note: After Q5, before results. Frame as delivery, not capture.

quiz_flow.results: Personalized top 3 recommendations with links to detailed reviews

post_quiz_email_series.email_2: The 5 mistakes first-time sauna buyers make
post_quiz_email_series.email_3: Our honest take: what we would buy with your budget

cta_strategy.optimal_count: One CTA, repeated 3-4 times on the page
key_stats: Pages with 1 CTA convert at 13.5%, vs 10.5% for 5+ CTAs.
Multiple competing offers decrease conversions by up to 266%.
CTAs above the fold outperform below-fold by 304%.

our_approach.single_action: Take the quiz / Find my sauna

analysis_paralysis_relief.the_insight: Our ICP is drowning. They have 47 tabs open. Every Reddit thread contradicts the last one. They do not need more information. They need someone to say: here is what to buy.

copywriting_techniques.name: Decision simplification
copywriting_techniques.how: Reduce the universe of options to 3. Not 10. Not 'it depends.' Three. With clear reasoning.
copywriting_techniques.example_copy: There are 200+ home saunas on the market. You need to see three.
copywriting_techniques.research: Jam study: 6 options = 30% purchase rate. 24 options = 3%. Fewer choices, more action.

copywriting_techniques.name: The trusted friend voice
copywriting_techniques.how: Write like someone who already went through this. Not an expert lecturing, a friend who bought one last year.
copywriting_techniques.example_copy: I spent three weeks doing exactly what you are doing right now. Here is what I wish someone had told me on day one.

copywriting_techniques.name: The recommended default
copywriting_techniques.how: Always highlight one option as 'our pick' or 'best for most people.' Defaults reduce cognitive load dramatically.
copywriting_techniques.example_copy: If you are not sure where to start, start here. This is the sauna we recommend to most people.

copywriting_techniques.name: Progressive disclosure
copywriting_techniques.how: Show essential info first. Hide specs behind expandable sections. Do not dump everything at once.
copywriting_techniques.application: Product cards show: name, price, one-line verdict, photo. Click to expand for specs, full review, comparison.

copywriting_techniques.name: Permission to stop researching
copywriting_techniques.how: Explicitly give them permission to close the other tabs. This is emotional, not logical.
copywriting_techniques.example_copy: You have done enough research. Seriously. Close the other tabs. We will take it from here.

copywriting_techniques.name: Risk reversal
copywriting_techniques.how: Make the next step feel safe. Not 'buy now' but 'get the free shortlist' or 'see your results.'
copywriting_techniques.example_copy: No credit card. No sales call. Just your top 3 matches, delivered to your inbox in 60 seconds.

page_copy_approach.headline: Stop researching. Start deciding.
alternative_headlines: You have read enough Reddit threads.
200+ saunas. You need to see three.
The honest home sauna guide. No brand deals. No BS.
We tested them so you do not have to.
page_copy_approach.opening_paragraph: You have been at this for weeks. You have read every Reddit thread. Watched the YouTube reviews. Compared spec sheets until your eyes glazed over. And you are no closer to buying a sauna than when you started. That ends here.

what_works.description: Casper succeeded by reducing mattress choice from hundreds to one. Simple copy, action-oriented CTAs ('try' not 'buy'), heavy social proof, free trial to remove risk.
what_works.application: We are not selling saunas. We are simplifying the decision. Our quiz narrows 200+ options to 3.

what_works.description: High-ticket home improvement pages use budget and property-type filters to qualify leads. This removes tire-kickers and makes serious buyers feel the experience is tailored to them.
what_works.application: Quiz questions about budget and space serve double duty: they qualify AND personalize.

what_works.description: Successful wellness landing pages use soothing colors (greens, blues, earth tones), nature imagery, generous white space, and calming micro-interactions.
what_works.application: Our design should feel like stepping into a sauna. Warm tones, wood textures, breathing room. Not a comparison spreadsheet.

what_works.description: Casper positioned themselves not as a mattress company but as a 'sleep company.' They built authority through educational content first, then converted readers to buyers.
what_works.application: We are not a sauna store. We are the independent sauna guide. This positions us as the trusted advisor, not another vendor.

recommended_page_structure.name: The Sauna Guide Buying Quiz Landing Page

sections_in_order.name: Hero
sections_in_order.purpose: Acknowledge their pain. Promise relief. Primary CTA.

copy_direction.headline: You have read enough Reddit threads.
copy_direction.subheadline: Take a 2-minute quiz. Get a personal shortlist of the 3 home saunas that actually fit your space, budget, and lifestyle.
copy_direction.trust_line: Free. No sales pitch. Based on 200+ hours of independent research.

sections_in_order.name: Problem (Agitate)
sections_in_order.purpose: Mirror their exact situation. Build empathy.
sections_in_order.copy_direction: Three short paragraphs about the research rabbit hole: contradicting Reddit advice, every brand claiming to be best, spec sheets that all look the same. End with: 'Sound familiar?'

sections_in_order.name: Guide credentials
sections_in_order.purpose: Establish why we can solve this. Not bragging, just facts.
sections_in_order.copy_direction: We are not a sauna brand. We do not sell saunas. We spent 200+ hours testing, comparing, and talking to owners so you get honest answers. Icons: X saunas reviewed, X brands compared, 0 brand sponsorships.

sections_in_order.name: The plan (3 steps)
sections_in_order.purpose: Make the path simple and clear.

sections_in_order.name: CTA repeat
sections_in_order.purpose: Second chance to convert before social proof.

sections_in_order.name: Social proof
sections_in_order.purpose: Testimonials from people who used the guide to buy.
sections_in_order.copy_direction: Short quotes. Real names if possible. Focus on the relief: 'I had been going back and forth for months. This quiz gave me clarity in 2 minutes.'

sections_in_order.name: Success vs Failure
sections_in_order.purpose: Paint two futures. Emotional, not logical.

copy_direction.with_us: Saturday morning. Steam rising. Your own sauna, 20 steps from your back door. You chose well.
copy_direction.without_us: Three more weeks of Reddit threads. Or worse: an $8,000 impulse buy you regret by spring.

sections_in_order.name: FAQ
sections_in_order.purpose: Handle objections. Keep them on page.

copy_direction.headline: Close the other tabs.
copy_direction.subheadline: You have done enough research. Let us help you decide.

adaptations: Our newsletter voice sells longing and stillness. The buying guide voice sells relief and clarity. Both are warm. Both are honest. The difference is the emotion: one invites you to slow down, the other gives you permission to stop overthinking.
Same rules apply: no em-dashes, no 'leveraging', no 'curating', short sentences, friend-over-coffee tone.
CTA language shifts slightly: newsletter uses 'Step inside.' Buying guide uses 'Find my sauna.' Both are invitations, not commands.
The buying guide can be more direct than the newsletter. People in buying mode want answers, not poetry. But we stay human.
